title: Method for infusing a beverage using a flavour pouch and device therefor
abstract: Method of infusing a beverage with flavoured material, the method comprising applying a plurality of pressure cycles on the flavoured material whilst in contact with a liquid to be infused, wherein each cycle comprising a sequence of consecutive pressure applications separated in time by an interval t1 of reduced pressure application, followed by a continuous period of reduced pressure application during a time interval t 2 , wherein is t 2 is larger than t1. Beverage infusion device (500) comprising: a beverage input (520), a beverage output (120) fluidly connected to the beverage input (520) by a flow path, and an infusion chamber (130) provided in said flow path, the infusion chamber defining a slot (550) configured to hold a pouch (100) filled with flavoured material, the slot being delimited at one side by a back wall and at the opposite side by a rotatable pressure disc (561) configured to engage with the pouch, wherein the pressure disc comprises distinct protrusions (560) on its surface facing the slot, the distinct protrusions are provided on a continuous segment of the disc extending over maximally 5/4 Pi radians and a continuous segment of the disc of at least 3/4 Pi radians is free of protrusions.
claims: Claims Claims 1. A method of infusing a beverage with flavoured material, the method comprising applying a plurality of pressure cycles on the flavoured material whilst in contact with a liq uid to be infused, wherein each cycle comprising a sequence of consecutive pressure applications separated in time by an interval ti of reduced pressure application, followed by a continuous period of reduced pressure application during a time interval t 2 , wherein is t 2 is larger than ti. 2. The method according to claim 1, wherein t 2 is at least 2*ti. 3. The method according to claim 1 or 2, comprising at least two, preferably three consecutive pressure applications in each cycle. 4. The method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the pressure is applied by mechanically compressing the flavoured material. 5. The method according to any of the preceding claims, comprising applying pressure on the flavoured material by means of a rotating disc comprising a first section with distinct spaced apart protruding pressure elements provided on a continuous segment of the disc extending over maximally 5/4 p radians and a second section free of pressure elements, said second section extending over a segment of at least 3/4 p radians of said disc. 6. A beverage infusion device comprising (i) a beverage input, (ii) a beverage output fluidly connected to the beverage input by a flow path, and (iii) an infusion chamber provided in said flow path, the infusion chamber defining a slot configured to hold a pouch filled with flavoured material, the slot delimited at one side by a back wall and at the opposite side by a rotatable pressure disc configured to engage with a pouch provided in the slot, characterised in that the pressure disc comprises distinct protrusions on its surface facing the slot, wherein the distinct protrusions are provided on a continuous segment of the disc extending over maximally 5/4 p radians and that a continuous segment of the disc of at least 3/4 p radians is free of protrusions. 7. The device according to claim 6, the protrusions configured as radial rims extending from the surface of the disc. 8. The device according to claim 6 or 7, the protrusions configured as rollers or series of rollers with their axis along radial lines in the disc, the rollers partially protruding from the surface of the disc. 9. The device according to any of the preceding claims 6 to 8, the back wall of the infusion chamber preferably comprises a design of several channels extending over it surface directed to the slot. 10. The device according to any of the preceding claims 6 to 9, comprising a tap valve arranged downstream of the infusion chamber. 11. The device according to any of the preceding claims 6 to 10, comprising a tap font wherein the infusion chamber is provided. 12. The device according to any of the preceding claims 6 to 11, the slot configured to house pouches with flavoured material is dimensioned to accommodate flavoured materials in an amount for a single serve tapping cycle only.
